category_title: Students with high Callous-Unemotional (CU) traits, proactive aggression, and reduced emotional responsiveness
minimal_context: The student is participating in playtime where another child is currently playing with a ball.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for playtime. You can go join the other children.

STUDENT: I want to play with the ball that boy is playing with.

TUTOR: He has the ball right now. What do you think you should do?

STUDENT: Well he was doing that as well.

TUTOR: Just go over there and try to get along.

STUDENT: (Hurts the other child to take the ball) If I hurt him, he will stop playing with the ball, then I can use it to start my own game of football.

options.A: Have more new motivating items, such as extra balls, readily available during the playtime session to offer the student.
options.B: Develop a play plan specific to the playtime theme: identify the theme (e.g., "Today we are playing football"), label the ball to be used, and ask the children how they could play with it.
options.C: Help the student compliment the other child and replace their hurtful actions with something more friendly during the playtime session.
options.D: Elicit the student's unhelpful thought that led to the wrong behavior, then help them recognize how their antisocial actions directly impact other people's thoughts and willingness to accommodate their wishes.
